spec_match.rationale: Uses prefixed (`ff_*`) keys and a composite `flag_revision` key — exactly the cardinality-control pattern the spec discussion recommends.

interactive.rationale: Toggleable flag grid that rewrites the payload live; one click crashes with the current set.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says toggle flags and watch keys rewrite; the page does that and adds the breakdown panel.

edge_cases.rationale: The cardinality-note prose is excellent but the page doesn't enforce it interactively — it lets you tag arbitrarily many flags without showing budget consumption.

references.rationale: Links sibling release-tagging concept but the chromestatus.com/feature URL needs confirming in the see-also (cut off in read).

openQuestions.title: Show budget cost per added flag
openQuestions.detail: Add a tiny budget meter ('17 of 50 keys used') that increments per ff_* key set, so the cardinality note in the prose becomes a felt constraint, not just advice.

openQuestions.title: Add a server-side resolution sketch
openQuestions.detail: The point of `flag_revision` is the dashboard resolves it server-side. Add a stub box that shows: 'flag_revision=8a3f → resolved to [ff_a:on, ff_b:off, ...]' so the reader sees the full pipeline, not just the wire half.

openQuestions.title: Confirm chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Invariant 3: chromestatus.com/feature/6228675846209536 must be in the see-also. Verify and add if missing.

summary: Cleanest of the crash-reporting concepts — picks a real SRE workflow and instruments it end to end. To go further, enforce the cardinality budget interactively and show server-side resolution.
